Paragraph.Text: any amount that would — in the absence of this subsection and if the insurer existed and carried on an insurance business on each day that is the day on which the amalgamation occurred or a subsequent day and on which the new corporation carries on an insurance business — be required to be deducted or included, in respect of any of those days, under subsection (18) or (19) in computing the insurer’s income from an insurance business.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 138(22) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection (23) applies if, at any time, an insurer (referred to in this subsection and subsection (23) as the “transferor”) transfers, to a corporation (referred to in this subsection and subsection (23) as the “transferee”) that is related to the transferor, property in respect of an insurance business carried on by the transferor (referred to in this subsection and subsection (23) as the “transferred business”) and
Paragraph 138(22)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 85(1) applies to the transfer, the transfer includes all or substantially all of the property and liabilities of the transferred business and, immediately after the transfer, the transferee carries on an insurance business.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 138(23)(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount that would — in the absence of this subsection and if the transferor existed and carried on an insurance business on each day that includes that time or is a subsequent day and on which the transferee carries on an insurance business — be required to be deducted or included, in respect of any of those days, under subsection (18) or (19) in computing the transferor’s income that can reasonably be attributed to the transferred business; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 138(24) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If at any time an insurer ceases to carry on all or substantially all of an insurance business (referred to in this subsection as the “discontinued business”), and none of subsections (20) to (22) apply,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: there shall be deducted, in computing the insurer’s income from the discontinued business for the insurer’s taxation year that includes the time that is immediately before that time, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount included under subsection (16) in computing the insurer’s income from the discontinued business for its transition year, and

is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under subsection (18) in computing the insurer’s income from the discontinued business for a taxation year that began before that time; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there shall be included, in computing the insurer’s income from the discontinued business for the insurer’s taxation year that includes the time that is immediately before that time, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount deducted under subsection (17) in computing the insurer’s income from the discontinued business for its transition year, and
is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount included under subsection (19) in computing the insurer’s income from the discontinued business for a taxation year that began before that time.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 138(25) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If at any time an insurer that carried on an insurance business ceases to exist (otherwise than as a result of a winding-up or amalgamation described in subsection (20) or (21)), for the purposes of subsection (24), the insurer is deemed to have ceased to carry on the insurance business at the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the time (determined without reference to this subsection) at which the insurer ceased to carry on the insurance business, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the time that is immediately before the end of the last taxation year of the insurer that ended at or before the time at which the insurer ceased to exist.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 138(26) of the Act is repealed.
Subsections (1) to (23) apply to taxation years that begin after 2022.
The portion of subsection 138.1(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

In respect of life insurance policies for which all or any part of an insurer’s reserves vary in amount depending on the fair market value of a specified group of properties that is reported to a relevant authority (as defined in subsection 138(12)) as a segregated fund (in this section referred to as a “segregated fund”), for the purposes of this Part, the following rules apply:
Subsection 138.1(7) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsections (1) to (6) do not apply to the holder of a segregated fund policy with respect to such a policy that is issued or effected as or under a FHSA, pooled registered pension plan, registered pension plan, registered retirement income fund, registered retirement savings plan or TFSA.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2022.
Subsection (2) comes into force on April 1, 2023.
The definition transition year in subsection 142.51(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: transition year of a taxpayer means the taxpayer’s first taxation year that begins after 2022. (année transitoire)

Subsection.Text: Subsections 142.51(2) and (3) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If a taxpayer is an insurer in its transition year, there shall be included in computing the taxpayer’s income for its transition year the absolute value of the negative amount, if any, of the taxpayer’s transition amount.

If a taxpayer is an insurer in its transition year, there shall be deducted in computing the taxpayer’s income for its transition year the positive amount, if any, of the taxpayer’s transition amount.
The portion of subsection 142.51(4) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

If an amount has been included under subsection (2) in computing a taxpayer’s income for its transition year, there shall be deducted in computing the taxpayer’s income for each particular taxation year of the taxpayer that ends after the beginning of the transition year, and in which particular taxation year the taxpayer is an insurer, the amount determined by the formula
The portion of subsection 142.51(5) of the Act before the formula is replaced by the following:

If an amount has been deducted under subsection (3) in computing a taxpayer’s income for its transition year, there shall be included in computing the taxpayer’s income, for each particular taxation year of the taxpayer ending after the beginning of the transition year, and in which particular taxation year the taxpayer is an insurer, the amount determined by the formula
The portion of subsection 142.51(6) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If a taxpayer has, in a winding-up to which subsection 88(1) has applied, been wound-up into another corporation (referred to in this subsection as the “parent”), and immediately after the winding-up the parent is an insurer, in applying subsections (4) and (5) in computing the income of the taxpayer and of the parent for particular taxation years that end on or after the first day (referred to in this subsection as the “start day”) on which assets of the taxpayer were distributed to the parent on the winding-up,
Subparagraph 142.51(6)(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount that would — in the absence of this subsection and if the taxpayer existed and was an insurer on each day that is the start day or a subsequent day and on which the parent is an insurer — be required to be deducted or included, in respect of any of those days, under subsection (4) or (5) in computing the taxpayer’s income for its transition year; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 142.51(7) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

If there is an amalgamation (within the meaning assigned by subsection 87(1)) of a taxpayer with one or more other corporations to form one corporation (referred to in this subsection as the “new corporation”), and immediately after the amalgamation the new corporation is an insurer, in applying subsections (4) and (5) in computing the income of the new corporation for particular taxation years of the new corporation that begin on or after the day on which the amalgamation occurred, the new corporation is, on and after that day, deemed to be the same corporation as and a continuation of the taxpayer in respect of
Paragraph 142.51(7)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any amount that would — in the absence of this subsection and if the taxpayer existed and was an insurer on each day that is the day on which the amalgamation occurred or a subsequent day and on which the new corporation is an insurer — be required to be deducted or included, in respect of any of those days, under subsection (4) or (5) in computing the taxpayer’s income.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 142.51(8)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 85(1) applies to the transfer, the transfer includes all or substantially all of the property and liabilities of the transferred business and, immediately after the transfer, the transferee is an insurer.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 142.51(9)(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: any amount that would — in the absence of this subsection and if the transferor existed and was an insurer on each day that includes that time or is a subsequent day and on which the transferee is an insurer — be required to be deducted or included, in respect of any of those days, under subsection (4) or (5) in computing the transferor’s income that can reasonably be attributed to the transferred business; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 142.51(10) of the Act is repealed.
The portion of subsection 142.51(11) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Ceasing to carry on a business
Subsection.Text: If at any time, a taxpayer ceases to be an insurer
The portion of subsection 142.51(12) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

If at any time a taxpayer ceases to exist (otherwise than as a result of a merger to which subsection 87(2) applies or a winding-up to which subsection 88(1) applies), for the purposes of subsection (11), the taxpayer is deemed to have ceased to be an insurer at the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the time (determined without reference to this subsection) at which the taxpayer ceased to be an insurer, and

Subsection.Text: Section 142.51 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (12):
Subsection (13.1) applies to a taxpayer for a particular taxation year of the taxpayer if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer holds a transition property in the particular taxation year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the property was a mark-to-market property of the taxpayer for the taxation year preceding the particular taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the property is not a mark-to-market property of the taxpayer for the particular taxation year.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies to a taxpayer for a particular taxation year of the taxpayer, for purposes of this section

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer is deemed to have ceased to be an insurer at the particular time that is the beginning of the particular taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the time immediately before the particular time shall be deemed to be the end of the taxation year that ends immediately before the particular taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (14) apply to taxation years that begin after 2022.
Subsection 146(16) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (a.1) and by adding the following after that paragraph:

Paragraph.Label: (a.2)
Paragraph.Text: to a FHSA for the benefit of the transferor, if subsection (8.3) would not apply to an amount in respect of the property in the case that the property was instead received by the transferor as a benefit out of or under the registered retirement savings plan, or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 146(16)(d) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: no deduction may be made under subsection (5), (5.1) or (8.2) or section 8, 60 or 146.6 in respect of the payment or transfer in computing the income of any taxpayer, and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) come into force on April 1, 2023.
Paragraph 146.3(2)(f) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of subparagraph (viii), by adding “or” at the end of subparagraph (ix) and by adding the following after subparagraph (ix):

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: a FHSA in accordance with subsection 146.6(7);

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on April 1, 2023.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 146.5:
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: annual FHSA limit of a taxpayer for a taxation year is the least of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all contributions made to a FHSA in the year by the taxpayer (other than any contributions made after the taxpayer’s first qualifying withdrawal from a FHSA),
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer’s maximum participation period has not begun in a preceding taxation year, nil, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount by which the amount determined under this paragraph for the preceding taxation year exceeds the annual FHSA limit for that taxation year, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all designated amounts described in paragraph (b) of the definition designated amount in subsection 207.01(1) for the year,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the FHSA carryforward for the taxation year;
is the total of all amounts transferred in the year or a preceding taxation year under paragraph 146(16)(a.2) to a FHSA under which the taxpayer is the holder, and
is the total of all amounts, each of which is an amount determined in respect of each preceding taxation year that is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer had not started their maximum participation period in the preceding taxation year, nil, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined for E in the preceding taxation year, and
is the amount determined for F in the preceding taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: $8,000 plus the amount of the FHSA carryforward for the preceding taxation year, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all designated amounts described in paragraph (a) of the definition designated amount in subsection 207.01(1), and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: nil, if the taxation year is after the year in which

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer’s maximum participation period has ended, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer has died. (plafond annuel au titre du CELIAPP)

Definition.Text: beneficiary under a FHSA means an individual (including an estate) or a qualified donee that has a right to receive a distribution from the FHSA after the death of the holder of the FHSA. (bénéficiaire)

first home savings account or FHSA means an arrangement registered with the Minister that has not ceased to be a FHSA under subsection 146.6(16). (compte d’épargne libre d’impôt pour l’achat d’une première propriété ou CELIAPP)
FHSA carryforward of a taxpayer for a taxation year is the least of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount determined in paragraph (b) of the definition annual FHSA limit for the preceding taxation year, and
is the amount determined in paragraph (a) of the definition annual FHSA limit for the preceding taxation year, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: nil, if the taxpayer had not started their maximum participation period prior to the taxation year. (montant des cotisations reporté)

Definition.Text: holder of an arrangement means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: until the death of the individual who entered into the arrangement, the individual; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: after the death of the individual, the individual’s survivor, if the survivor is designated under the arrangement to become a successor of the holder and is a qualifying individual. (titulaire)

Definition.Text: issuer of an arrangement means the person described as the issuer in the definition qualifying arrangement. (émetteur)
maximum participation period of an individual means the period that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: begins when an individual first enters into a qualifying arrangement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ends at the end of the year following the year in which the earliest of the following events occur:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the 14th anniversary of the date the individual first enters into a qualifying arrangement,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual attains 70 years of age, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual first makes a qualifying withdrawal from a FHSA. (période de participation maximale)

Definition.Text: non-qualified investment has the same meaning as in subsection 207.01(1). (placement non admissible)
qualified investment has the same meaning as in subsection 207.01(1). (placement admissible)
qualifying arrangement, at a particular time, means an arrangement

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is entered into after March 2023 between a person (in this definition referred to as the “issuer”) and a qualifying individual;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: an arrangement in trust with an issuer that is a corporation licensed or otherwise authorized under the laws of Canada or a province to carry on in Canada the business of offering to the public its services as trustee,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an annuity contract with an issuer that is a licensed annuities provider, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a deposit with an issuer that is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a person that is, or is eligible to become, a member of the Canadian Payments Association, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a credit union that is a shareholder or member of a body corporate referred to as a “central” for the purposes of the Canadian Payments Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: that provides for contributions to be made under the arrangement to the issuer in consideration of, or to be used, invested or otherwise applied for the purpose of, the issuer making distributions under the arrangement to the holder;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: under which the issuer and the qualifying individual agree, at the time the arrangement is entered into, that the issuer will file with the Minister an election to register the arrangement as a FHSA, in the prescribed form and manner under the Social Insurance Number of the qualifying individual with whom the arrangement was entered into; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: that, at all times throughout the period that begins at the time the arrangement is entered into and that ends at the particular time, complies with the conditions in subsection (2). (arrangement admissible)

Definition.Text: qualifying home means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a housing unit located in Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a cooperative housing corporation, the holder of which is entitled to possession of a housing unit located in Canada, except that, where the context so requires, a reference to a share with a right to possession of a housing unit described means the housing unit to which the share relates. (habitation admissible)

Definition.Text: qualifying individual, at a particular time, means an individual who

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is a resident of Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is at least 18 years of age; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: did not, at any prior time in the calendar year or in the preceding four calendar years, inhabit as a principal place of residence a qualifying home (or what would be a qualifying home if it were located in Canada) that was owned, whether jointly with another person or otherwise, by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the individual, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person who is the spouse or common-law partner of the individual at the particular time. (particulier déterminé)

Definition.Text: qualifying withdrawal of an individual means an amount received at a particular time by the individual as a benefit out of or under a FHSA if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount is received as a result of the individual’s written request in prescribed form in which the individual sets out the location of a qualifying home that the individual has begun, or intends not later than one year after its acquisition by the individual to begin, using as a principal place of residence;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the individual

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is a resident of Canada throughout the period that begins at the particular time and ends at the earlier of the time of the individual’s death and the time at which the individual acquires the qualifying home, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: does not have an owner-occupied home within the meaning of paragraph 146.01(2)(a.1) in the period

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: that begins at the beginning of the fourth preceding calendar year that ended before the particular time, and

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: that ends on the 31st day before the particular time;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the individual entered into an agreement in writing before the particular time for the acquisition or construction of the qualifying home before October 1 of the calendar year following the year in which the amount was received; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the individual did not acquire the qualifying home more than 30 days before the particular time. (retrait admissible)

Definition.Text: survivor of a qualifying individual means another individual who is, immediately before the qualifying individual’s death, a spouse or common-law partner of the qualifying individual. (survivant)

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (e) of the definition qualifying arrangement in subsection (1), the conditions are as follows:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement requires that it be maintained for the exclusive benefit of the holder (determined without regard to any right of a person to receive a payment out of or under the arrangement only on or after the death of the holder);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement prohibits, while there is a holder of the arrangement, anyone that is neither the holder nor the issuer of the arrangement from having rights under the arrangement relating to the amount and timing of distributions and the investing of funds;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement prohibits anyone other than the holder from making contributions under the arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement permits distributions to be made to reduce the amount of tax otherwise payable by the holder under section 207.021;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement provides that, at the direction of the holder, the issuer shall transfer all or any part of the property held in connection with the arrangement (or an amount equal to its value) to another FHSA of the holder or to an RRSP or a RRIF under which the holder is the annuitant;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: if the arrangement is an arrangement in trust, it prohibits the trust from borrowing money or other property for the purposes of the arrangement;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement provides that it ceases to be a FHSA after the end of the holder’s maximum participation period;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement, if it involves an issuer described in subparagraph (b)(iii) of the definition qualifying arrangement in subsection (1), includes provisions stipulating that the issuer has no right of offset with respect to the property held under the arrangement in connection with any debt or obligation owing to the issuer; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the arrangement meets prescribed conditions.

Subsection.Text: No tax is payable under this Part by a trust that is governed by a FHSA on its taxable income for a taxation year, except that, if at any time in the taxation year, it carries on one or more businesses or holds one or more properties that are non-qualified investments for the trust, tax is payable under this Part by the trust on the amount that would be its taxable income for the taxation year if it had no incomes or losses from sources other than those businesses and properties, and no capital gains or capital losses other than from dispositions of those properties, and for that purpose,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: income includes dividends described in section 83;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the trust’s taxable capital gain or allowable capital loss from the disposition of a property is equal to its capital gain or capital loss, as the case may be, from the disposition; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the trust’s income shall be computed without reference to subsection 104(6).

Subsection.Text: If tax is payable under this Part for a taxation year by application of subsection (3) by a trust that is governed by a FHSA that carries on one or more businesses at any time in the taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the holder of the FHSA is jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable with the trust to pay each amount payable under this Act by the trust that is attributable to that business or those businesses; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the issuer’s liability at any time for amounts payable under this Act in respect of that business or those businesses may not exceed the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of property of the trust that the issuer is in possession or control of at that time in its capacity as legal representative of the trust, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the total amount of all distributions of property from the trust on or after the date that the notice of assessment was sent in respect of the taxation year and before that time.

Subsection.Text: There may be deducted in computing a taxpayer’s income for a taxation year an amount not exceeding the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the taxpayer’s annual FHSA limit for the year and each preceding taxation year, and
is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount deducted under this subsection in computing the individual’s income for preceding taxation years, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount by which $40,000 exceeds the total of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined for B in paragraph (a), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: all amounts transferred in the year or a preceding taxation year under paragraph 146(16)(a.2) to a FHSA under which the taxpayer is the holder.

Subsection.Text: There shall be included in computing the income of a taxpayer for a taxation year the total of all amounts received by the taxpayer in the year out of or under a FHSA of which the taxpayer is the holder, other than an amount that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a qualifying withdrawal;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a designated amount as defined in subsection 207.01(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: otherwise included in computing the income of the taxpayer.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (8) applies to an amount transferred at a particular time from a FHSA (in this subsection referred to as the “transferor FHSA”) if the following conditions are met:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount is transferred on behalf of an individual who is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the holder of the transferor FHSA,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a spouse or common-law partner or former spouse or common-law partner of the holder of the transferor FHSA and who is entitled to the amount under a decree, order or judgment of a competent tribunal, or under a written agreement, relating to a division of property between the holder and the individual, in settlement of rights arising out of, or on a breakdown of, their marriage or common-law partnership, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: entitled to the amount as a consequence of the death of the holder of the transferor FHSA and was a spouse or common-law partner of the holder immediately before the death;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount is transferred directly to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: another FHSA of the individual, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an RRSP or a RRIF under which the individual is the annuitant; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the transfer is not made to another FHSA of the holder of the transferor FHSA, the amount does not exceed the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount that is the total fair market value, immediately before the particular time, of all property held by a FHSA under which the holder of the transferor FHSA is a holder, and
is the excess FHSA amount (as defined in subsection 207.01(1)) of the holder of the transferor FHSA at the particular time.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies to an amount transferred from a FHSA,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount shall not, by reason only of the transfer, be included in computing the income of any taxpayer; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: no deduction may be made under this Part in respect of the amount in computing the income of any taxpayer.

Subsection.Text: If an amount is transferred from a FHSA to a plan or fund (in this subsection referred to as the “transferee plan”) that is a FHSA, RRSP or RRIF and subsection (8) does not apply to the amount transferred,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount is deemed to have been received from the FHSA by the holder of the FHSA;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the holder or annuitant of the transferee plan is deemed to have paid the amount as a contribution or premium to the transferee plan; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case that the transferee plan is a RRIF, for the purposes of subsection 146(5) and Part X.1, the annuitant of the transferee plan is deemed to have paid the amount at the time of the transfer as a premium under a RRSP under which the annuitant is the annuitant (as defined in subsection 146(1)).

Subsection.Text: If an amount is transferred from a FHSA to another FHSA, or to a RRSP or RRIF, and a portion but not all of the amount is transferred in accordance with subsection (7),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (8) applies to the portion of the amount transferred in accordance with subsection (7), and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (9) applies with respect to the remainder of the amount.

Subsection.Text: If at any time in a taxation year a trust governed by a FHSA uses or permits to be used any property of the trust as security for a loan, the fair market value of the property at the time it commenced to be so used shall be included in computing the income for the year of the holder of the FHSA at that time.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Recovery of property used as security
Subsection.Text: If in a taxation year a property described in subsection (11) ceases to be used as security for a loan, there may be deducted, in computing the income of the holder of the relevant FHSA for the taxation year, an amount equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount included by application of subsection (11) in computing the income of the holder as a consequence of the property being used as security for a loan; and

is the net loss (exclusive of payments by the trust as or on account of interest) sustained by the trust in consequence of its using the property, or permitting it to be used, as security for the loan and not as a result of a change in the fair market value of the property.

Subsection.Text: If the holder of a FHSA dies and the holder’s survivor is designated as the successor holder of the FHSA, the survivor is, immediately after the time of death, deemed to have entered into a new qualifying arrangement in respect of the FHSA unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the survivor is a qualifying individual and the balance of the FHSA is transferred to a RRSP or a RRIF of the survivor, or distributed to the survivor in accordance with subsection (14), by the end of the year following the year of death; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the survivor is not a qualifying individual, in which case the balance of the FHSA is to be transferred to a RRSP or a RRIF of the survivor, or distributed to the survivor in accordance with subsection (14), by the end of the year following the year of death.

Subsection.Text: If, as a consequence of the death of the holder of a FHSA, an amount is distributed in a taxation year from the FHSA to, or on behalf of, a beneficiary, the amount shall be included in computing the beneficiary’s income for the year.

If an amount is distributed at any time from the FHSA of a deceased holder to the holder’s legal representative and a survivor of the holder is entitled to all or a portion of the amount in full or partial satisfaction of the survivor’s rights as a person beneficially interested under the deceased’s estate, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a payment is made from the estate to a FHSA, RRSP or RRIF of the survivor, the payment is deemed to be a transfer from the FHSA to the extent that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it is so designated jointly by the legal representative and the survivor in prescribed form filed with the Minister, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it meets the conditions to be a transferred amount under subsections (7) to (10);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a payment is made from the estate to the survivor, the payment is deemed for the purposes of subsection (14) to be a distribution to the survivor as a beneficiary to the extent that it is so designated jointly by the legal representative and the survivor in prescribed form filed with the Minister; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection (14), the amount distributed to the legal representative from the FHSA is deemed to be reduced by the amounts designated in paragraphs (a) and (b).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Arrangement ceasing to be a FHSA
Subsection.Text: An arrangement that was filed with the Minister as a FHSA ceases to be a FHSA at

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (b), the earliest of the following times:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the end of the maximum participation period of the last holder,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the end of the year following the year of the death of the last holder,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the time at which the arrangement ceases to be a qualifying arrangement, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the time at which the arrangement is not administered in accordance with the conditions in subsection (2); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a later time specified by the Minister in writing.

Subsection.Text: If an arrangement ceases at a particular time to be a FHSA,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (3) does not apply to exempt the trust governed by the arrangement from tax under this Part on the taxable income of the trust earned after the particular time;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer who was the holder under the arrangement is not deceased at the particular time, an amount equal to the fair market value of all property of the arrangement immediately before the particular time is to be included in the taxpayer’s income for the taxation year that includes the particular time; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the last holder is deceased at the particular time, each beneficiary of the FHSA shall include in their income, for the taxation year that includes the particular time, the proportion of the fair market value of all property of the arrangement immediately before the particular time that the beneficiary is entitled to.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations requiring issuers of FHSAs to file information returns in respect of FHSAs.
Subsection (1) comes into force on April 1, 2023.
Subsection 148(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (b.3):

Paragraph.Label: (b.4)
Paragraph.Text: a FHSA,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on April 1, 2023.
Subsection 149(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (u.3):

Paragraph.Label: (u.4)
Paragraph.Text: a trust governed by a FHSA to the extent provided by section 146.6;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on April 1, 2023.
The definition disbursement quota in subsection 149.1(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: disbursement quota, for a taxation year of a registered charity, means the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the number of days in the taxation year, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 3.5% of the prescribed amount for the year, in respect of all or a portion of a property owned by the charity at any time in the 24 months immediately preceding the taxation year that was not used directly in charitable activities or administration, if the prescribed amount is equal to or less than $1 million but greater than

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the registered charity is a charitable organization, $100,000, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, $25,000,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the prescribed amount for the year in respect of all or a portion of a property owned by the charity at any time in the 24 months immediately preceding the taxation year that was not used directly in charitable activities or administration is greater than $1 million, $35,000 plus 5% of the amount by which the prescribed amount exceeds $1 million, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (c)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, nil; (contingent des versements)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 149.1(1.1) of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of paragraph (a), by adding “and” at the end of paragraph (c) and by adding the following after paragraph (c):

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: expenditures on administration and management of the charity.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 149.1(4.1)(d) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: of a registered charity, if it has in a taxation year received a gift of property (other than a designated gift) from another registered charity with which it does not deal at arm’s length and it has expended, before the end of the next taxation year, in addition to its disbursement quota for each of those taxation years, an amount that is less than the fair market value of the property, on charitable activities carried on by it or by way of gifts that are qualifying disbursements to qualified donees or grantee organizations, with which it deals at arm’s length;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 149.1(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Minister may, on application made to the Minister in prescribed form by a registered charity, specify an amount in respect of the charity for a taxation year and the registered charity’s disbursement quota shall be deemed to be reduced by that amount.
Subsection 149.1(8) of the Act is repealed.
Subsections (1), (2) and (4) apply to taxation years beginning on or after January 1, 2023.
Subsection (3) is deemed to have come into force on June 23, 2022.
Subsection (5) applies in respect of applications made on or after January 1, 2023.
The portion of subsection 150(1.1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (1.2), subsection (1) does not apply to a taxation year of a taxpayer if
Section 150 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1.1):

Subsection (1.1) does not apply to a taxation year of a trust if the trust is resident in Canada and is an express trust, or for civil law purposes a trust other than a trust that is established by law or by judgement, unless the trust

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: had been in existence for less than three months at the end of the year;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: holds assets with a total fair market value that does not exceed $50,000 throughout the year, if the only assets held by the trust throughout the year are one or more of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: money,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a debt obligation described in paragraph (a) of the definition fully exempt interest in subsection 212(3),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a share, debt obligation or right listed on a designated stock exchange,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a share of the capital stock of a mutual fund corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a unit of a mutual fund trust,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: an interest in a related segregated fund trust (within the meaning assigned by paragraph 138.1(1)(a)), and

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: an interest as a beneficiary under a trust, all the units of which are listed on a designated stock exchange;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: is required under the relevant rules of professional conduct or the laws of Canada or a province to hold funds for the purposes of the activity that is regulated under those rules or laws, provided the trust is not maintained as a separate trust for a particular client or clients;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: is a registered charity;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: is a club, society or association described in paragraph 149(1)(l);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: is a mutual fund trust;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: is, for greater certainty, a related segregated fund trust, within the meaning assigned by paragraph 138.1(1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: is a trust, all the units of which are listed on a designated stock exchange;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: is prescribed to be a master trust;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: is, for greater certainty, a graduated rate estate;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: is a qualified disability trust, as defined in subsection 122(3);

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: is an employee life and health trust;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: is a trust described under paragraph 81(1)(g.3);

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: is a trust under or governed by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a deferred profit sharing plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a pooled registered pension plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered disability savings plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered education savings plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered pension plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered retirement income fund,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered retirement savings plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: a tax-free savings account,

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: an employee profit sharing plan,

Subparagraph.Label: (x)
Subparagraph.Text: a registered supplementary unemployment benefit plan, or

Subparagraph.Label: (xi)
Subparagraph.Text: a first home savings account; or

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: is a cemetery care trust or a trust governed by an eligible funeral arrangement.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, a trust includes an arrangement under which a trust can reasonably be considered to act as agent for all the beneficiaries under the trust with respect to all dealings with all of the trust’s property.
For greater certainty, subsections (1.1) to (1.3) do not require the disclosure of information that is subject to solicitor-client privilege.
Subsections (1) and (2) apply to taxation years that end after December 30, 2023.
Subsection 152(1.11) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If at any time the Minister ascertains the tax consequences to a taxpayer because of subsection 245(2) with respect to a transaction, the Minister

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shall, in the case of a determination under subsection 245(8), determine any amount that is, or could at a subsequent time be, relevant for the purposes of computing the income, taxable income or taxable income earned in Canada of, tax or other amount payable by, or amount refundable to, the taxpayer under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: may, in any case not described in paragraph (a), determine any amount referred to in paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: shall, if a determination is made under this subsection, send to the taxpayer, with all due dispatch, a notice of determination stating the amount so determined.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 152(4)(b) of the Act is amended by adding the following after subparagraph (v):

Subparagraph.Label: (v.1)
Subparagraph.Text: is made in respect of an amount deducted under subsection 127(5) in respect of a flow-through critical mineral mining expenditure as defined in subsection 127(9),

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of determinations made on or after April 7, 2022. For greater certainty, determinations made under subsection 152(1.11) of the Act prior to April 7, 2022 continue to be binding, to the extent provided under subsection 152(1.3) of the Act.
Subsection (2) is deemed to have come into force on April 7, 2022.
Subsection 153(1) of the Act is amended by striking out “or” at the end of paragraph (t), by adding “or” at the end of paragraph (u) and by adding the following after paragraph (u):

Paragraph.Label: (v)
Paragraph.Text: a payment out of or under

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a FHSA, if the amount is required by section 146.6 to be included in computing a taxpayer’s income, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an arrangement that ceased to be a FHSA by application of subsection 146.6(16)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) comes into force on April 1, 2023.
Section 160 of the Act is amended by adding the following before subsection (1):
In this section and section 160.01, a transaction includes an arrangement or event.
Paragraph 160(1)(d) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: d)
Paragraph.Text: le bénéficiaire du transfert et l’auteur du transfert sont solidairement responsables du paiement d’une partie de l’impôt de l’auteur du transfert en vertu de la présente partie pour chaque année d’imposition égale à l’excédent de l’impôt pour l’année sur ce que cet impôt aurait été sans l’application des articles 74.1 à 75.1 de la présente loi et de l’article 74 de la Loi de l’impôt sur le revenu, chapitre 148 des Statuts revisés du Canada de 1952, à l’égard de tout revenu tiré des biens ainsi transférés ou des biens y substitués ou à l’égard de tout gain tiré de la disposition de tels biens;

Subsection.Text: The portion of paragraph 160(1)(e) of the French version of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: e)
Paragraph.Text: le bénéficiaire du transfert et l’auteur du transfert sont solidairement responsables du paiement en vertu de la présente loi d’un montant égal au moins élevé des montants suivants :

Subsection.Text: Section 160 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

For the purposes of subsections (1) to (4), if a person (referred to in this section as the “transferor”) has transferred property either directly or indirectly, by means of a trust or by any other means whatever to another person (referred to in this section as the “transferee”) in a transaction or as part of a series of transactions

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor is deemed to not be dealing at arm’s length with the transferee at all times in the transaction or series of transactions if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: at any time during the period beginning immediately prior to the transaction or series of transactions and ending immediately after the transaction or series of transactions, the transferor and transferee do not deal at arm’s length, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes of undertaking or arranging the transaction or series of transactions is to avoid joint and several, or solidary, liability of the transferee and transferor for an amount payable under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that the transferor is liable to pay under this Act (including, for greater certainty, an amount that the transferor is liable to pay under this section, regardless of whether the Minister has made an assessment under subsection (2) for that amount) is deemed to have become payable in the taxation year in which the property was transferred if it is reasonable to conclude that one of the purposes for the transfer of property is to avoid the payment of a future amount payable under this Act by the transferor or transferee; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the amount determined under subparagraph (1)(e)(i) is deemed to be the greater of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount otherwise determined under that subparagraph without reference to this paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value of the property at the time of the transfer, and
is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the lowest fair market value of the consideration (that is held by the transferor) given for the property at any time during the period beginning immediately prior to the transaction or series of transactions and ending immediately after the transaction or series of transactions, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the consideration is in a form that is cancelled or extinguished during the period referred to in clause (A),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (I)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount that is the lowest of the amount determined in clause (A) and the fair market value during the period of any property, other than property that is cancelled or extinguished during the period, that is substituted for the consideration referred to in clause (A), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (II)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if there is no property that is substituted for the consideration referred to in clause (A), other than property cancelled or extinguished during the period, nil.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) to (4) are deemed to have come into force on April 19, 2021.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 160:
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: gross entitlements of a person at any time, in respect of a planning activity of the person, means all amounts to which the person, or another person not dealing at arm’s length with the person, is entitled, either before or after that time and either absolutely or contingently, to receive or obtain in respect of the activity. (droits à paiement)
person includes a partnership. (personne)
planning activity has the same meaning as in subsection 163.2(1). (activité de planification)
section 160 avoidance planning by a person, means planning activity in respect of a transaction or series of transactions that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is, or is part of, a section 160 avoidance transaction; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: one of the purposes of the transaction or series of transactions is to

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: reduce a transferee’s joint and several, or solidary, liability for tax owing under this Act by the transferor (or that would be owing by the transferor if not for a tax attribute transaction), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: reduce the person’s or another person’s ability to pay any amount owing, or that may become owing, under this Act. (planification d’évitement en vertu de l’article 160)

Definition.Text: section 160 avoidance transaction means a transaction or series of transactions in respect of which

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions set out in paragraph 160(5)(a) or (b) are met; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if subsection 160(5) applied to the transaction or series of transactions, the amount determined under subparagraph 160(5)(c)(ii) would exceed the amount determined under subparagraph 160(5)(c)(i). (opération d’évitement en vertu de l’article 160)

Definition.Text: tax attribute means a balance, pool or other amount determined under this Act that is or may be relevant in computing income or in determining a taxpayer’s liability for tax under this Act in any taxation year and includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a capital loss, non-capital loss, restricted farm loss, farm loss and limited partnership loss;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that is deductible in computing a person’s income;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any balance of undeducted outlays, expenses or other amounts;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: paid-up capital in respect of a share of any class of the capital stock of a corporation;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: cost or capital cost of a property;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: an amount deductible from an amount otherwise payable under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: an amount that is deemed to have been remitted as an amount payable under this Act. (attribut fiscal)

Definition.Text: tax attribute transaction means a transaction or series of transactions in which a tax attribute – of a person that dealt at arm’s length with a transferor or transferee immediately before the transaction or series of transactions – is used, directly or indirectly, to provide a tax benefit for the transferor or transferee (or, if either the transferor or transferee is amalgamated with another corporation, the new corporation within the meaning assigned by subsection 87(1)). (opération d’attribut fiscal)
tax benefit has the same meaning as in subsection 163.2(1). (avantage fiscal)
transferee has the meaning assigned by subsections 160(1) and (5). (bénéficiaire du transfert)
transferor has the meaning assigned by subsections 160(1) and (5). (auteur du transfert)

Subsection.Text: Every person that engages in, participates in, assents to or acquiesces in planning activity that they know is section 160 avoidance planning, or would reasonably be expected to know is section 160 avoidance planning, but for circumstances amounting to gross negligence is liable to a penalty that is the lesser of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 50% of the amount payable under this Act (determined without reference to this subsection), the joint and several, or solidary, liability for which was sought to be avoided through the planning, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of $100,000 and the person’s gross entitlements at the time at which the notice of assessment of the penalty is sent to the person in respect of the planning.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) does not apply to a person solely because the person provided clerical services or secretarial services with respect to the planning.
Subsection (1) is deemed to apply in respect of a transaction or a series of transactions that occurs, all or in part, after April 18, 2021.
Section 160.2 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2.2):

If an amount required to be included in the income of a holder of a FHSA because of section 146.6 is received by a taxpayer other than the holder, that taxpayer is jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable with the holder to pay a part of the holder’s tax under this Part for the taxation year in which the amount is received equal to the amount by which the holder’s tax for the year exceeds the amount that would be the holder’s tax for the year if the amount had not been received, but nothing in this subsection limits the liability of the holder under any other provision of this Act or of the taxpayer for the interest that the taxpayer is liable to pay under this Act on an assessment in respect of the amount that the taxpayer is liable to pay because of this subsection.
Subsection 160.2(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If a taxpayer and an annuitant or holder have, by virtue of subsection (1), (2) or (2.3), become jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable in respect of part or all of a liability of the annuitant or holder under this Act, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a payment by the taxpayer on account of the taxpayer’s liability shall to the extent thereof discharge their liability; but

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a payment by the annuitant or holder on account of the liability of the annuitant or holder discharges the taxpayer’s liability only to the extent that the payment operates to reduce the liability of the annuitant or holder to an amount less than the amount in respect of which the taxpayer was, by subsection (1), (2) or (2.3), as the case may be, made jointly and severally, or solidarily, liable.

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) come into force on April 1, 2023.
Paragraphs 161(1)(a) to (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the total of the taxpayer’s taxes payable under this Part and Parts I.3, VI, VI.1 and VI.2 (determined in accordance with subsection 191.5(9)) for the year

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is an amount paid at or before that time on account of the taxpayer’s tax payable and applied as at that time by the Minister against the taxpayer’s liability for an amount payable under this Part or Part I.3, VI, VI.1 or VI.2 for the year,

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2022 and subsequent taxation years.

Section.MarginalNote: False statement or omission — trust return

Subsection.Text: Section 163 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):
A person or partnership is liable to a penalty if the person or partnership

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: knowingly or under circumstances amounting to gross negligence

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: makes — or participates in, assents to or acquiesces in, the making of — a false statement or omission in a return of income of a trust that is not subject to one of the exceptions listed in paragraphs 150(1.2)(a) to (o) for a taxation year, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: fails to file a return described in subparagraph (i); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: fails to comply with a demand under subsection 150(2) or 231.2(1) to file a return described in subparagraph (a)(i).

Subsection.MarginalNote: False statement or omission — trust return
Subsection.Text: The amount of the penalty to which the person or partnership is liable under subsection (5) is equal to the greater of

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 5% of the highest amount at any time in the year that is equal to the total fair market value of all the property held by the trust referred to in subsection (5) at that time.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that end after December 30, 2023.
Subsection 181(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this Part, the expressions attributed surplus, Canadian assets, Canadian premiums, Canadian reserve liabilities, contractual service margin, group of insurance contracts, group of reinsurance contracts, permanent establishment, policyholders’ liabilities, reinsurance contract held amount, total assets, total premiums and total reserve liabilities have such meanings as may be prescribed.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2022.
Paragraphs 181.3(3)(b) and (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an insurance corporation that was resident in Canada at any time in the year and carried on a life insurance business at any time in the year, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the corporation’s long-term debt at the end of the year,
is the total amount, at the end of the year, of the corporation’s

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: capital stock (or, in the case of an insurance corporation incorporated without share capital, the amount of its members’ contributions),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: retained earnings,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: accumulated other comprehensive income,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: policyholders’ liabilities,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (v)
FormulaParagraph.Text: contributed surplus, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vi)
FormulaParagraph.Text: any other surpluses,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the contractual service margin for a group of insurance contracts of the corporation at the end of the year other than a group of segregated fund policies,
is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount, in respect of a group of reinsurance contracts held by the corporation at the end of the year, that is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if no portion of the contractual service margin for the group is in respect of a risk under a segregated fund policy, the contractual service margin for the group, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount that would be the contractual service margin for the group if the contractual service margin were determined excluding any portion of the contractual service margin that is in respect of the reinsurance of risks under segregated fund policies, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of any deficit deducted in computing the shareholders’ equity (including, for this purpose, the amount of any provision for the redemption of preferred shares) at the end of the year;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an insurance corporation that was resident in Canada at any time in the year and throughout the year did not carry on a life insurance business, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the corporation’s long-term debt at the end of the year,
is the total amount, at the end of the year, of the corporation’s

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: capital stock (or, in the case of an insurance corporation incorporated without share capital, the amount of its members’ contributions),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: retained earnings,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: accumulated other comprehensive income,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: policyholders’ liabilities,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (v)
FormulaParagraph.Text: contributed surplus, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vi)
FormulaParagraph.Text: any other surpluses,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is total of all amounts each of which is the contractual service margin for a group of insurance contracts of the corporation at the end of the year that is in respect of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: non-cancellable or guaranteed renewable accident and sickness policies in respect of accident and sickness insurance (as defined in subsection 1408(1) of the Income Tax Regulations),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: mortgage insurance (as defined in subsection 1408(1) of the Income Tax Regulations), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: title insurance (as defined in subsection 1408(1) of the Income Tax Regulations),

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount, in respect of a group of reinsurance contracts held by the corporation at the end of the year, that is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the contractual service margin for the group, if no portion of the contractual service margin is in respect of a risk under an insurance policy other than an insurance policy that is in respect of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: non-cancellable or guaranteed renewable accident and sickness policies in respect of accident and sickness insurance (as defined in subsection 1408(1) of the Income Tax Regulations),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: mortgage insurance (as defined in subsection 1408(1) of the Income Tax Regulations), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (C)
FormulaParagraph.Text: title insurance (as defined in subsection 1408(1) of the Income Tax Regulations), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount that would be the contractual service margin for the group if the contractual service margin were determined excluding any portion that is in respect of the reinsurance of risks under policies other than those described in any of clauses (i)(A) to (C),

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the corporation’s reserves for the year, except to the extent that they

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: were deducted in computing its income under Part I for the year, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: are reserves in respect of the contractual service margin for a group of insurance contracts of the corporation at the end of the year,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the reinsurance contract held amount for a group of reinsurance contracts held by the corporation at the end of the year, to the extent the amount can be reasonably regarded as being included in the amount determined under the description of E, and
is the amount of any deficit deducted in computing the shareholders’ equity (including, for this purpose, the amount of any provision for the redemption of preferred shares) at the end of the year;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 181.3(3)(d)(iv) of the Act is amended by adding “and” at the end of clause (C) and by replacing clauses (D) to (F) with the following:

Clause.Label: (F)
Clause.Text: the total of all amounts each of which is the reinsurance contract held amount for a group of reinsurance contracts held by the corporation at the end of the year, to the extent the amount can be reasonably regarded as being included in the amount determined under clause (A); and

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (2) apply to taxation years that begin after 2022.
Subsection 190(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: contractual service margin for a group of insurance contracts of an insurer, or a group of reinsurance contracts held by the insurer, at the end of a taxation year, has the same meaning as in subsection 138(12); (marge sur services contractuels)
group of insurance contracts of an insurer has the same meaning as in subsection 138(12); (groupe de contrats d’assurance)
group of reinsurance contracts held by an insurer has the same meaning as in subsection 138(12); (groupe de contrats de réassurance)
group of segregated fund policies of an insurer has the same meaning as in subsection 138(12); (groupe de polices à fonds réservé)
policyholders’ liabilities of an insurer at the end of a taxation year has the same meaning as in subsection 138(12); (obligation envers les titulaires de polices)

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2022.
Paragraph 190.1(3)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the corporation’s tax payable under Parts I and VI.2 (determined in accordance with subsection 191.5(9)) for the year; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2022 and subsequent taxation years.
Paragraph 190.13(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a life insurance corporation that was resident in Canada at any time in the year, the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the corporation’s long-term debt at the end of the year,
is the total amount, at the end of the year, of the corporation’s

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: capital stock (or, in the case of an insurance corporation incorporated without share capital, the amount of its members’ contributions),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: retained earnings,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: accumulated other comprehensive income,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: policyholders’ liabilities,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (v)
FormulaParagraph.Text: contributed surplus, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (vi)
FormulaParagraph.Text: any other surpluses,

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the contractual service margin for a group of insurance contracts of the corporation at the end of the year other than a group of segregated fund policies,
is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount, in respect of a group of reinsurance contracts held by the corporation at the end of the year, that is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if no portion of the contractual service margin for the group is in respect of a risk under a segregated fund policy, the contractual service margin for the group, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount that would be the contractual service margin for the group if the contractual service margin were determined excluding any portion that is in respect of the reinsurance of risk under a segregated fund policy, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of any deficit deducted in computing the shareholders’ equity (including, for this purpose, the amount of any provision for the redemption of preferred shares) at the end of the year;

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after 2022.
The Act is amended by adding the following after Part VI.1:
The following definition applies in this Part.

Definition.Text: bank or life insurer group member means a corporation that is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a bank;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a life insurance corporation that carries on business in Canada; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a financial institution (as defined in subsection 190(1)) that is related to any corporation described in paragraph (a) or (b). (membre d’un groupe de banques ou d’assureurs-vie)

Subsection.Text: Every corporation that is a bank or life insurer group member at any time during a 2021 taxation year shall pay a tax under this Part for its 2022 taxation year equal to the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the corporation’s total taxable income (or taxable income earned in Canada if the corporation is non-resident) for

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 2020 taxation years of the corporation, determined under Part I, without regard to paragraphs 111(1)(a) and (b), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: 2021 taxation years of the corporation, determined under Part I, without regard to paragraphs 111(1)(a) and (b); and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the corporation is not related to another bank or life insurer group member at the end of each 2021 taxation year of the corporation, $1 billion, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, the amount determined under subsection (7).

Subsection.Text: If a corporation has more than one 2022 taxation year, the latest 2022 taxation year is the 2022 taxation year for the purposes of subsection (2).
For the purposes of subsection (2),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a corporation has more than one 2020 taxation year and the aggregate number of days in all its 2020 taxation years is greater than 365 days, the amount determined for the corporation under paragraph (a) of the description of A in subsection (2) shall be reduced to that proportion of that amount that 365 is of the aggregate number of days in all 2020 taxation years; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if a corporation has more than one 2021 taxation year and the aggregate number of days in all its 2021 taxation years is greater than 365 days, the amount determined for the corporation under paragraph (b) of the description of A in subsection (2) shall be reduced to that proportion of that amount that 365 is of the aggregate number of days in all 2021 taxation years.

Subsection.Text: A corporation that is described in paragraph (a) or (b) of the definition bank or life insurer group member in subsection (1) at any time during a 2021 taxation year and that was related to any other bank or life insurer group member at the end of the year (in this section, the corporation and each such bank or life insurer group member are referred to together as a “related group”) may file with the Minister an agreement in prescribed form, with the prescribed form referred to in subsection (8), on behalf of the related group under which an amount that does not exceed $1 billion is allocated among the members of the related group for the taxation year.

The Minister may request a corporation that is member of a related group at any time during the 2021 taxation year to file with the Minister an agreement referred to in subsection (5) and, if the corporation does not file the agreement within 30 days after receiving the request, the Minister may allocate the amount referred to in subsection (5) among the members of the related group for the taxation year.

For the purposes of this Part, the least amount allocated for the taxation year to each bank or life insurer group member under an agreement described in subsection (5) or by the Minister under subsection (6) is the amount determined for B in subsection (2) for the taxation year of that member, but, if no such allocation is made, the amount determined for B in subsection (2) of each bank and life insurer group member for the year is nil.

A corporation that is a bank or life insurer group member shall file with the Minister, no later than the day on or before which the corporation is required by section 150 to file its return of income for the 2022 taxation year under Part I, a prescribed form containing prescribed information.

A corporation liable to pay tax for the 2022 taxation year under this Part shall pay 1/5 of the tax to the Receiver General on or before its balance-due day for the 2022 and each of the four subsequent taxation years.

Section.Text: Sections 152, 158 and 159, subsection 161(11), sections 162 to 167 and Division J of Part I apply to this Part with such modifications as the circumstances require.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2022 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection 204.6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If at the end of any month a taxpayer that is a registered investment described in paragraph 204.4(2)(b), (d) or (f) holds property that is not a prescribed investment for that taxpayer, it shall, in respect of that month, pay a tax under this Part equal to the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined in respect of such a property by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the fair market value of the property at the time of its acquisition by the taxpayer;
is the total number of units or shares of the capital stock of the registered investment held at the end of the month by one or more

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: trusts that are governed by any of a RDSP, RESP, RRIF, RRSP, TFSA or deferred profit sharing plan, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: registered investments described in paragraph 204.4(2)(b), (d) or (f); and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total number of issued units or issued and outstanding shares of the capital stock of the registered investment at the end of the month.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (a) of the description of B in subsection 204.6(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: trusts that are governed by any of a FHSA, RDSP, RESP, RRIF, RRSP, TFSA or deferred profit sharing plan, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of months after December 2020. It also applies in respect of months before 2021 if, on or before April 19, 2021,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: no notice of assessment in respect of an amount payable under subsection 204.6(1) of the Act for the month had been sent to the taxpayer in respect of the month; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if such a notice of assessment had been sent to the taxpayer in respect of the month on or before that date, the taxpayer had rights of objection or appeal in respect of the assessment on that date.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies in respect of months after March 2023.
The portion of subsection 207.01(1) of the Act before the first definition is replaced by the following:

The following definitions and the definitions in subsections 146(1) (other than the definition benefit), 146.1(1), 146.2(1), 146.3(1), 146.4(1) and 146.6(1) apply in this Part and Part XLIX of the Income Tax Regulations.
The definition registered plan in subsection 207.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: registered plan means a FHSA, RDSP, RESP, RRIF, RRSP or TFSA. (régime enregistré)
